title: NOC 31301 – Registered Nurses and Registered Psychiatric Nurses | GTR Immigration
description: Registered Nurses (RNs) and Registered Psychiatric Nurses (RPNs) under NOC 31301 are among the most in-demand healthcare professionals in Canada. Driven by healthcare system expansion, workforce shortages, and population aging, nursing remains a priority occupation for federal and provincial immigration programs. With strong salary levels, long-term employment stability, and clear regulatory pathways, NOC 31301 provides one of the most reliable routes toward Canada PR.

classification.teer: Classification LevelDetailsBroad Occupation Category3 – Health occupationsTEER1 – Occupations usually require a university degreeMajor Group31 – Professional occupations in healthSub-Major Group313 – Nursing and allied health professionalsMinor Group3130 – Nursing and allied health professionalsNOC Code31301

duties: Registered Nurses and Registered Psychiatric Nurses perform some or all of the following duties:
Assess patients and identify nursing interventions
Assist in surgical and medical procedures
Provide nursing services in business and industrial settings
Deliver public health services and immunization programs
Conduct home visits and manage complex care cases
Provide psychiatric and mental health nursing care
Deliver supportive counseling and life skills programming
Provide leadership for specialized patient groups
Support implementation of research-based care practices
employment_requirements: Completion of an approved registered nursing program
Additional specialization training may be required
Master’s or doctoral degree typically required for advanced roles
Mandatory registration with provincial regulatory authority
Completion of an approved psychiatric nursing program
Registration required in Manitoba, Saskatchewan, Alberta, British Columbia, and Yukon
British Columbia College of Nurses and Midwives
College and Association of Registered Nurses of Alberta
Ordre des infirmières et infirmiers du Québec
Internationally educated nurses must complete credential assessment and licensing exams before practicing.
salary_prospects: Annual earnings typically range from $52,000 to $94,500, depending on province, experience, and specialty.
job_outlook: The outlook for NOC 31301 is strong nationwide. With over 155,000 projected openings, nursing remains one of the largest and most stable occupational groups in Canada’s labour market.
Internationally educated nurses who complete credential recognition and licensing requirements have excellent long-term employment prospects.
labour_market_demand: Registered Nurses and Registered Psychiatric Nurses are employed in:

This significant gap reflects sustained national demand. High-demand provinces include Ontario, Alberta, British Columbia, Saskatchewan, Manitoba, and northern regions such as Northwest Territories and Nunavut.
Why Immigrate to Canada Under NOC 31301?
Competitive wages and unionized salary structures
Job security in public healthcare systems
Family settlement benefits including universal healthcare
Nursing remains one of Canada’s most immigration-supported occupations.
pathways: If you are a qualified nursing professional, NOC 31301 provides a high-demand and immigration-supported pathway to Canada.
